{\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n157\t//\n158\t// Parameters:\n159\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n160\t//   - message: The core error message\n161\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n162\t//\n163\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n164\t//\n165\t// Example usage:\n166\t//\n167\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n169\t//\n170\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n180\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n181\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\t// Add field name if present\n185\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n203\t//\n204\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n205\t//\n206\t// Example usage:\n207\t//\n208\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n343\t//\n344\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n345\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n346\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n347\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n348\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// =============================================================================\n352\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n353\t// =============================================================================\n354\t\n355\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n356\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n357\t//\n358\t// Parameters:\n359\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n360\t//\n361\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n362\t//\n363\t// Example usage:\n364\t//\n365\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n366\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n370\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n371\t\tswitch category {\n372\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n395\t//\n396\t// Parameters:\n397\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n398\t//\n399\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n400\t//\n401\t// Example usage:\n402\t//\n403\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n404\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n453\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n462\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n463\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n464\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n465\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n466\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n467\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n468\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n481\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n482\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n486\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n487\t//\n488\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n489\t//\n490\t// Example usage:\n491\t//\n492\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n493\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n494\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n495\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n496\t//\t}\n497\t//\n498\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n499\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n500\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n501\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n502\t\n503\t\t// Add severity if requested\n504\t\tif includeSeverity {\n505\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n506\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n507\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n508\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n509\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n510\t\t\t} else {\n511\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n512\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n513\t\t\t}\n514\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\t// Add error type and field\n518\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n519\t\n520\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n521\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n522\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n523\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n524\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n525\t\t\t}\n526\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n527\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n528\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n530\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n531\t\t\t}\n532\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\t// Add location if present\n536\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n537\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\treturn builder.String()\n541\t}\n542\t\n543\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n544\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n545\t//\n546\t// Parameters:\n547\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n548\t//\n549\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n550\t//\n551\t// Example usage:\n552\t//\n553\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n554\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n555\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n556\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n557\t//\t}\n558\t//\n559\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n560\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n561\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n562\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n563\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n564\t\t}\n565\t\n566\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n567\t}\n568\t\n569\t// =============================================================================\n570\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n571\t// =============================================================================\n572\t\n573\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n574\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n575\t//\n576\t// Parameters:\n577\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n578\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n579\t//\n580\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n581\t//\n582\t// Example usage:\n583\t//\n584\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n585\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n586\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n587\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n588\t//\t}, {\n589\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n590\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n591\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n592\t//\t}}\n593\t//\n594\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n595\t//\t// Returns:\n596\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n597\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n598\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n599\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n600\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\tvar lines []string\n604\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n605\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n606\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n607\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n608\t\t}\n609\t\n610\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n614\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n615\t//\n616\t// Parameters:\n617\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n618\t//\n619\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n620\t//\n621\t// Example usage:\n622\t//\n623\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n624\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n625\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n626\t//\t}, {\n627\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n628\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n629\t//\t}}\n630\t//\n631\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n632\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n633\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n634\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n635\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\t// Count errors by type\n639\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n640\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n641\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n642\t\t}\n643\t\n644\t\t// Build summary parts\n645\t\tvar parts []string\n646\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n647\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// =============================================================================\n654\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n655\t// =============================================================================\n656\t\n657\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n658\t// without the parent parameter.\n659\t//\n660\t// Parameters:\n661\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n662\t//   - location: Additional location context\n663\t//\n664\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n665\t//\n666\t// Example usage:\n667\t//\n668\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n669\t//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\n670\tfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n671\t\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n672\t}\n673\t\n674\t// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n675\t// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n676\t//\n677\t// Parameters:\n678\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n679\t//\n680\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n681\t//\n682\t// Example usage:\n683\t//\n684\t//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n685\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n686\tfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n687\t\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n688\t}\n689\t\n690\t// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n691\t// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n692\t//\n693\t// Parameters:\n694\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n695\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n696\t//\n697\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n698\tfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n699\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n700\t}\n701\t\n702\t// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n703\t//\n704\t// Parameters:\n705\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n706\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n707\t//\n708\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n709\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n710\t\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n711\t}\n712\t\n713\t// =============================================================================\n714\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n715\t// =============================================================================\n716\t\n717\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n718\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n719\t//\n720\t// Parameters:\n721\t//   - value: The value to quote\n722\t//\n723\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n724\t//\n725\t// Example usage:\n726\t//\n727\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n728\t//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n729\t//\n730\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n731\t//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\n732\tfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n733\t\tif value == \"\" {\n734\t\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n738\t}\n739\t\n740\t// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n741\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n742\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n743\t//\n744\t// Parameters:\n745\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n746\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n747\t//\n748\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n749\t//\n750\t// Example usage:\n751\t//\n752\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n753\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n754\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n755\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n756\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n757\t\t}\n758\t\n759\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n760\t\t\treturn value\n761\t\t}\n762\t\n763\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n764\t}\n765\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_9bb63d1192054a02b6daca48","session_id":"941279a7-b347-40d2-b507-e9ad55ac8a75","uuid":"79978766-3b0f-4a42-8a61-6bff434abfd9","timestamp":"2026-07-14T16:42:14.425Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find FormatError and ErrorType structures"}

details.\n89\t// Use this to provide granular information about what was checked and what failed.\n90\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter =============================================================================\n130\t\n131\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n132\t//\n133\t// Parameters:\n134\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n135\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n136\t//   - context: Optional context FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n193\t//\n194\t// Parameters:\n195\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n196\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n197\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n200\t//\n201\t// Example usage:\n202\t//\n203\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n204\t//\t// Output:\n205\t//\t// status_code_range parameters for errors\n211\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n212\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n213\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n214\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n215\t\n216\t\t// Build range info string\n217\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n218\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n219\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tdetails := []string{}\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\tdetails = append(details, FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n273\t//\n274\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n275\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n276\t// your specific requirements.\n277\t//\n278\t// Parameters:\n279\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n280\t//   - expected: The expected value\n281\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n282\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n283\t//\n284\t// Example usage:\n285\t//\n286\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n287\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n288\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n289\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n290\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n291\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n292\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n293\t//\t)\n294\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n295\t\tvalidationType string,\n296\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n297\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n298\t) ValidationError {\n299\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n300\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n301\t\t\topt(config)\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n305\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n306\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n307\t\n308\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n309\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n315\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n321\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n324\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n325\t\t}\n326\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n327\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// =============================================================================\n334\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n335\t// =============================================================================\n336\t\n337\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n338\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n339\t\tContext            string\n340\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n341\t\tFieldName          string\n342\t\tPatternDetails     string\n343\t\tRangeInfo          string\n344\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n345\t\tSuggestions        []string\n346\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n347\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n348\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n349\t\tStatusCode         int\n350\t\tTimeout            int\n351\t\tSecurityContext    string\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n355\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n356\t\n357\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n358\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n359\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n360\t\t\tc.Context = context\n361\t\t}\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n365\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n366\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n367\t\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n368\t\t}\n369\t}\n370\t\n371\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\n372\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n373\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n374\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n375\t\t}\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n379\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n380\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n381\t\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n382\t\t}\n383\t}\n384\t\n385\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n386\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n387\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n388\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n389\t\t}\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n393\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n394\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n395\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n396\t\t}\n397\t}\n398\t\n399\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\n400\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n401\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n402\t\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n403\t\t}\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t// WithSeverityOverride creates a FormatOption that overrides the default severity level.\n407\t// Use this when you want to specify a custom severity instead of using the error type's default.\n408\t//\n409\t// Example usage:\n410\t//\n411\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n412\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n413\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n414\t//\t    \"email\",\n415\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n416\t//\t)\n417\tfunc WithSeverityOverride(severity ErrorSeverity) FormatOption {\n418\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n419\t\t\tc.SeverityOverride = severity\n420\t\t}\n421\t}\n422\t\n423\t// WithCategoryHint creates a FormatOption that provides a category hint for formatting.\n424\t// Use this when you want to specify a custom category instead of using the error type's default.\n425\t//\n426\t// Example usage:\n427\t//\n428\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n429\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n430\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n431\t//\t    \"email\",\n432\t//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n433\t//\t)\n434\tfunc WithCategoryHint(category ErrorCategory) FormatOption {\n435\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n436\t\t\tc.CategoryHint = category\n437\t\t}\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t// WithStatusCode creates a FormatOption that sets the HTTP status code for HTTP errors.\n441\t// Use this with HTTP-related errors to include status code information in the formatted message.\n442\t//\n443\t// Example usage:\n444\t//\n445\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n446\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n447\t//\t    \"Resource not found\",\n448\t//\t    \"endpoint\",\n449\t//\t    validate.WithStatusCode(404),\n450\t//\t)\n451\tfunc WithStatusCode(statusCode int) FormatOption {\n452\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n453\t\t\tc.StatusCode = statusCode\n454\t\t}\n455\t}\n456\t\n457\t// WithTimeout creates a FormatOption that sets the timeout for performance errors.\n458\t// Use this with performance-related errors to include timeout information in the formatted message.\n459\t//\n460\t// Example usage:\n461\t//\n462\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n463\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n464\t//\t    \"Request timed out\",\n465\t//\t    \"api_response\",\n466\t//\t    validate.WithTimeout(5000),\n467\t//\t)\n468\tfunc WithTimeout(timeout int) FormatOption {\n469\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n470\t\t\tc.Timeout = timeout\n471\t\t}\n472\t}\n473\t\n474\t// WithSecurityContext creates a FormatOption that sets the security context for security errors.\n475\t// Use this with security-related errors to include security context in the formatted message.\n476\t//\n477\t// Example usage:\n478\t//\n479\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n480\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n481\t//\t    \"Invalid credentials\",\n482\t//\t    \"Authorization\",\n483\t//\t    validate.WithSecurityContext(\"authentication\"),\n484\t//\t)\n485\tfunc WithSecurityContext(securityContext string) FormatOption {\n486\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n487\t\t\tc.SecurityContext = securityContext\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t\n492\t// =============================================================================\n493\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n494\t// =============================================================================\n495\t\n496\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n497\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n498\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n499\t\tif err != nil {\n500\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n504\t\tif err != nil {\n505\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n506\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n507\t\t}\n508\t\n509\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n510\t}\n511\t\n512\t// =============================================================================\n513\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n514\t// =============================================================================\n515\t\n516\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n517\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n518\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n519\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n520\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n521\t}\n522\t\n523\t// global tracker instance\n524\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n525\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n526\t}\n527\t\n528\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n529\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n530\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n531\t//\n532\t// Parameters:\n533\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n534\t//\n535\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n536\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n537\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n538\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n539\t\n540\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n541\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n542\t}\n543\t\n544\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n545\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n546\t// configuration issues.\n547\t//\n548\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n549\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n550\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n551\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n552\t\n553\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n554\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n555\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n556\t\t}\n557\t\treturn result\n558\t}\n559\t\n560\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n561\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n562\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n563\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n564\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n565\t\n566\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n567\t}\n568\t\n569\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n570\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n571\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n572\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n573\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n574\t\n575\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n576\t}\n577\t\n578\t// =============================================================================\n579\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n580\t// =============================================================================\n581\t\n582\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n583\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n584\t//\n585\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n586\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n587\t//\n588\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n589\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n590\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n591\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n592\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n593\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n594\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n595\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n596\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n597\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n598\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n599\t//\n600\t// Parameters:\n601\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n602\t//   - message: The error message content\n603\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n604\t//\n605\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n606\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n607\t//\n608\t// Example usage:\n609\t//\n610\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n611\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n612\t//\n613\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n614\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n615\t//\n616\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n617\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n618\t//\n619\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n620\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n621\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n622\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n623\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n624\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n625\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n626\t\n627\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n628\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n629\t\n630\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n631\t\tif message == \"\" {\n632\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n633\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n634\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n635\t\t\t} else {\n636\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n637\t\t\t}\n638\t\t}\n639\t\n640\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n641\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n642\t}\n643\t\n644\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n645\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n646\t//\n647\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n648\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n649\t//\n650\t// Error Type Validation:\n651\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n652\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n653\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n654\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n655\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n656\t//\n657\t// Parameters:\n658\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n659\t//   - message: The error message content\n660\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n661\t//\n662\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n663\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n664\t//\n665\t// Example usage:\n666\t//\n667\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n668\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n669\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n670\t//\n671\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n672\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n673\t//\n674\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n675\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n676\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n677\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n678\t//\n679\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n680\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n681\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n682\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n683\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n684\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n685\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n686\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n687\t\t}\n688\t\n689\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n690\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n691\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n692\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n693\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n694\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n695\t\n696\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n697\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n698\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n699\t\t}\n700\t\n701\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n702\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n703\t\n704\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n705\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n706\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n707\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n708\t\n709\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n710\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n711\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n712\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n713\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n714\t\t}\n715\t\n716\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n717\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n718\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n719\t\n720\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n721\t\tif message == \"\" {\n722\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n723\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n724\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n725\t\t\t} else {\n726\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n727\t\t\t}\n728\t\t}\n729\t\n730\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n731\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n732\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n733\t\t}\n734\t\n735\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n736\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n737\t}\n738\t\n739\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n740\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n741\t//\n742\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n743\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n744\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n745\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n746\t}\n747\t\n748\t// =============================================================================\n749\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n750\t// =============================================================================\n751\t\n752\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n753\ttype QuoteStyle string\n754\t\n755\tconst (\n756\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n757\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n758\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n759\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n760\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n761\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n762\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n763\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n764\t)\n765\t\n766\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n767\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n768\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n769\t\tprefix     string\n770\t}\n771\t\n772\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n773\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n774\t\n775\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n776\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n777\t//\n778\t// Example usage:\n779\t//\n780\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n781\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n782\t//\n783\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n784\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n785\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n786\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n787\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n788\t\t}\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n792\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n793\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n794\t//\n795\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n796\t//\n797\t// Example usage:\n798\t//\n799\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n800\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n801\t//\n802\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n803\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n804\t//\n805\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n806\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n807\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n808\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n809\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n810\t\t}\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\t// =============================================================================\n814\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n815\t// =============================================================================\n816\t\n817\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n818\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n819\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n820\t//\n821\t// Parameters:\n822\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n823\t//\n824\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n825\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n826\t//\n827\t// Example usage:\n828\t//\n829\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n830\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n831\t//\n832\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n833\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n834\t//\n835\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n836\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n837\t//\n838\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n839\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n840\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n841\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n842\t\n843\t\t// Handle empty path\n844\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n845\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n846\t\t}\n847\t\n848\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n849\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n853\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n854\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n855\t\tif path == \"\" {\n856\t\t\treturn \"\"\n857\t\t}\n858\t\n859\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n860\t\tvar result []string\n861\t\n862\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n863\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n864\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n865\t\t\t\tcontinue\n866\t\t\t}\n867\t\n868\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n869\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n870\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n871\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n872\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n873\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n874\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n875\t\t\t\t} else {\n876\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n877\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n878\t\t\t\t}\n879\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n880\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n881\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n882\t\t\t} else {\n883\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n884\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n885\t\t\t}\n886\t\t}\n887\t\n888\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n889\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n890\t\t}\n891\t\n892\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n893\t}\n894\t\n895\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n896\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n897\t\tif s == \"\" {\n898\t\t\treturn false\n899\t\t}\n900\t\n901\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n902\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n903\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n904\t\t\t\treturn false\n905\t\t\t}\n906\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n907\t\t}\n908\t\n909\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n910\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n911\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n912\t\t\t\treturn false\n913\t\t\t}\n914\t\t}\n915\t\n916\t\treturn true\n917\t}\n918\t\n919\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n920\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n921\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n922\t}\n923\t\n924\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n925\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n926\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n927\t// for field names through variadic options.\n928\t//\n929\t// Parameters:\n930\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n931\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n932\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n933\t//\n934\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n935\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n936\t//\n937\t// Example usage:\n938\t//\n939\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n940\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n941\t//\n942\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n943\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n944\t//\n945\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n946\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n947\t//\n948\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n949\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n950\t//\n951\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n952\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n953\t//\n954\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n955\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n956\t//\n957\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n958\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n959\t//\n960\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n961\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n962\t//\n963\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n964\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n965\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n966\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n967\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n968\t\n969\t\t// Apply options\n970\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n971\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n972\t\t\topt(config)\n973\t\t}\n974\t\n975\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n976\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n977\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n978\t\t}\n979\t\n980\t\t// Handle empty field path\n981\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n982\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n983\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n984\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n985\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n986\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n987\t\t\t\t}\n988\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n989\t\t\t}\n990\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n991\t\t}\n992\t\n993\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n994\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n995\t\n996\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n997\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n998\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n999\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1000\t\t\t}\n1001\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1002\t\t}\n1003\t\n1004\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1005\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1006\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1007\t\t}\n1008\t\n1009\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1010\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1011\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1012\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1013\t\n1014\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1015\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1016\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1017\t\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1020\t\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1023\t}\n1024\t\n1025\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1026\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1027\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1028\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1029\t\t\treturn path\n1030\t\t}\n1031\t\n1032\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1033\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1034\t\tinBrackets := false\n1035\t\n1036\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1037\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1038\t\n1039\t\t\tswitch {\n1040\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1041\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1042\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1043\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1044\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1045\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1046\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1047\t\t\t\t}\n1048\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1049\t\n1050\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1051\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1052\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1053\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1054\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1055\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1056\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1057\t\t\t\t}\n1058\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1059\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1060\t\n1061\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1062\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1063\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1064\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1065\t\n1066\t\t\tdefault:\n1067\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1068\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1069\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1070\t\t\t\t} else {\n1071\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1072\t\t\t\t}\n1073\t\t\t}\n1074\t\t}\n1075\t\n1076\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1077\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1078\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1079\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1080\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1081\t\t}\n1082\t\n1083\t\treturn result.String()\n1084\t}\n1085\t\n1086\t// =============================================================================\n1087\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1088\t// =============================================================================\n1089\t\n1090\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1091\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1092\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1093\t//\n1094\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1095\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1096\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1097\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1098\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1099\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1100\t//\n1101\t// Parameters:\n1102\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1103\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1104\t//   - message: The error message content\n1105\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1106\t//\n1107\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1108\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1109\t//\n1110\t// Example usage:\n1111\t//\n1112\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1113\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1114\t//\n1115\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1116\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1117\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1118\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1119\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1120\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1121\t\t}\n1122\t\n1123\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1124\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1125\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1126\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1127\t\n1128\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1129\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1130\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1131\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1132\t\t\t} else {\n1133\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1134\t\t\t}\n1135\t\t}\n1136\t\n1137\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1138\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1139\t\n1140\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1141\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1142\t\n1143\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1144\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1145\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1146\t\n1147\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1148\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1149\t\n1150\t\t// Add field name if present\n1151\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1152\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1153\t\t}\n1154\t\n1155\t\t// Add message\n1156\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1157\t\n1158\t\treturn builder.String()\n1159\t}\n1160\t\n1161\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1162\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1163\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1164\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1165\t\n1166\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1167\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1168\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1169\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1170\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1171\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1172\t\t}\n1173\t\n1174\t\treturn \"\"\n1175\t}\n1176\t\n1177\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1178\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1179\t\tswitch severity {\n1180\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1181\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1182\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1183\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1184\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n1185\t\t\treturn \"MED\"\n1186\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n1187\t\t\treturn \"LOW\"\n1188\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n1189\t\t\treturn \"INFO\"\n1190\t\tdefault:\n1191\t\t\treturn \"UNK\"\n1192\t\t}\n1193\t}\n1194\t\n1195\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1196\t// both category and severity awareness. This is the most comprehensive formatting\n1197\t// function that respects both error categorization and severity levels.\n1198\t//\n1199\t// This function applies category-specific formatting:\n1200\t// - HTTP errors: Technical focus with status code details\n1201\t// - Content errors: Response structure and body focus\n1202\t// - Validation errors: Field-level validation focus\n1203\t// - Performance errors: Timing and rate limit focus\n1204\t// - Security errors: Authentication and authorization focus\n1205\t//\n1206\t// Combined with severity-based styling for maximum clarity.\n1207\t//\n1208\t// Parameters:\n1209\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n1210\t//   - message: The error message content\n1211\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1212\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization\n1213\t//\n1214\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity awareness.\n1215\t//\n1216\t// Example usage:\n1217\t//\n1218\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1219\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1220\t//\t    \"Field is validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1224\t//\t)\n1225\t//\t// Returns: \"[🚨 CRIT] [Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n1226\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1227\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1228\t\tmessage string,\n1229\t\tfieldName string,\n1230\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1231\t) string {\n1232\t\t// Get default category and severity\n1233\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1234\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1235\t\n1236\t\t// Apply options to override defaults\n1237\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1238\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1239\t\t\topt(config)\n1240\t\t}\n1241\t\n1242\t\t// Apply overrides if specified\n1243\t\tif config.SeverityOverride != \"\" {\n1244\t\t\tseverity = config.SeverityOverride\n1245\t\t}\n1246\t\tif config.CategoryHint != \"\" {\n1247\t\t\tcategory = config.CategoryHint\n1248\t\t}\n1249\t\n1250\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1251\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1252\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1253\t\n1254\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1255\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1256\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1257\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1258\t\t\t} else {\n1259\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1260\t\t\t}\n1261\t\t}\n1262\t\n1263\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1264\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1265\t\n1266\t\t// Build CategoryCustom && category != \"\" {\n1278\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(categoryPrefix)\n1279\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1280\t\t}\n1281\t\n1282\t\t// Add error type in {\n1287\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1288\t\t}\n1289\t\n1290\t\t// Add message\n1291\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1292\t\n1293\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n1294\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n1295\t\n1296\t\treturn builder.String()\n1297\t}\n1298\t\n1299\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n1300\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n1301\t\tswitch category {\n1302\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1303\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n1304\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n1305\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n1306\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n1307\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n1308\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1309\t\t\treturn \"[Performance]\"\n1310\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1311\t\t\treturn \"[Security]\"\n1312\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n1313\t\t\treturn \"[Custom]\"\n1314\t\tdefault:\n1315\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1316\t\t}\n1317\t}\n1318\t\n1319\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n1320\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n1321\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n1322\t\n1323\t\tswitch category {\n1324\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1325\t\t\t// Add HTTP-specific context\n1326\t\t\tif config.StatusCode != 0 {\n1327\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", config.StatusCode))\n1328\t\t\t}\n1329\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1330\t\t\t// Add performance-specific context\n1331\t\t\tif config.Timeout > 0 {\n1332\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (timeout: %dms)\", config.Timeout))\n1333\t\t\t}\n1334\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1335\t\t\t// Add security-specific context\n1336\t\t\tif config.SecurityContext != \"\" {\n1337\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (security: %s)\", config.SecurityContext))\n1338\t\t\t}\n1339\t\t}\n1340\t\n1341\t\treturn suffix.String()\n1342\t}\n1343\t\n1344\t// =============================================================================\n1345\t// CATEGORY-SPECIFIC FORMATTING FUNCTIONS\n1346\t// =============================================================================\n1347\t\n1348\t// FormatHTTPError creates a formatted error message specifically for HTTP-related errors.\n1349\t// This \")\n1369\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n1370\t\n1371\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1372\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1413\t\t}\n1414\t\n1415\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1416\t\n1417\t\tif expected != nil || actual != nil {\n1418\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n1419\t\t\tif expected != nil type (e.g., \"timeout\", \"rate_limit\")\n1439\t//   - timeoutMs: The timeout in milliseconds (use 0 if not applicable)\n1440\t//   - message: The error message\n1441\t//   - fieldName: Optional field
